grants.title: Maternal and Child Health Services Title V Block Grant Program
grants.summary: Funds initiatives to improve maternal and child health outcomes, including reducing infant mortality and addressing health disparities.
grants.description: The Maternal and Child Health Services Title V Block Grant Program supports efforts to improve the health of mothers, infants, and children, including children with special health care needs. The program focuses on reducing infant mortality, addressing racial and ethnic disparities, and promoting safe sleep practices. Eligible applicants include state health departments and organizations working in maternal and child health. Applications typically require detailed proposals outlining planned interventions and expected outcomes.
grants.eligibility_notes: State health departments and organizations focused on maternal and child health are eligible to apply.
key_requirements: Focus on maternal and child health outcomes
Address racial and ethnic disparities
Promote safe sleep practices
Provide detailed intervention plans

grants.title: Clinical Guideline for the Diagnosis, Evaluation and Management of Adults and Children with Asthma
grants.summary: This grant funds the development and dissemination of clinical guidelines for asthma care in adults and children.
grants.description: The purpose of this grant is to support the creation of evidence-based guidelines for diagnosing, evaluating, and managing asthma in adults and children. The guidelines aim to improve patient outcomes by providing recommendations for assessment, monitoring, education, environmental control, and medication use. Beneficiaries include healthcare providers and patients with asthma. Applications should focus on research, implementation, and education efforts related to asthma care.
grants.eligibility_notes: Eligible applicants include healthcare organizations, research institutions, and public health agencies.
key_requirements: Applicants must focus on asthma care and management.
Proposals should include evidence-based approaches.
Organizations must demonstrate capacity for guideline implementation.

grants.title: Safety Net Transformation Program
grants.summary: Supports hospitals and clinics in New York State to improve healthcare access and quality for underserved populations.
grants.description: The Safety Net Transformation Program is designed to assist hospitals and diagnostic and treatment centers (D&TCs) in New York State in enhancing healthcare services for underserved communities. The program focuses on improving access to care, quality of services, and operational efficiency. Eligible facilities include hospitals, hospital extension clinics, and free-standing clinics such as Federally Qualified Health Centers (FQHCs) and Rural Health Centers (RHCs). Applications can be submitted through the Health Commerce System (HCS). Detailed guidance and requirements are provided under Title 10 NYCRR regulations.
grants.eligibility_notes: Eligible applicants include hospitals, hospital extension clinics, and free-standing clinics such as FQHCs and RHCs in New York State.
key_requirements: Must be a hospital or clinic in New York State
Focus on underserved populations
Compliance with Title 10 NYCRR regulations

grants.title: School-Based Health Centers in New York State
grants.summary: This program funds the establishment and operation of school-based health centers to provide primary and preventive health care services to underserved children and youth in New York State.
grants.description: The School-Based Health Centers (SBHCs) program aims to improve access to primary and preventive health care for children and youth in low-income, high-risk communities. Established in 1981, the program utilizes state, federal, and private foundation funds to provide comprehensive health services through multidisciplinary teams. Services include physical and mental health assessments, chronic disease management, health education, and referrals. Eligible students must attend schools with SBHCs and have signed parental consent forms. Interested parties can contact the School Health Unit for more information or to apply.
grants.eligibility_notes: Organizations such as community health centers, hospitals, and school districts can apply to establish SBHCs. All children and youth in schools with SBHCs are eligible for services with signed parental consent.
key_requirements: Organizations must be community health centers, hospitals, or school districts.
Applicants must demonstrate the ability to provide comprehensive health services.
Parental consent is required for children to receive services.

grants.title: Single Source Procurements
grants.summary: Funding opportunities for various health-related programs and initiatives in New York State.
grants.description: The Single Source Procurements provide funding for a wide range of health-related programs and services in New York State. These include initiatives focused on disease prevention, health equity, technical assistance, and community health improvement. Beneficiaries include individuals, families, and organizations working in healthcare and public health. Applications are typically tied to specific contracts and deadlines, with detailed information available on the procurement archive page.
grants.eligibility_notes: Eligibility is specific to each program and contract, typically targeting healthcare organizations and public health entities.
key_requirements: Must be a healthcare or public health organization

Applications must adhere to specific contract guidelines

grants.title: Contracts Reportable Under Executive Order No. 6
grants.summary: This grant funds contracts for personal services that meet specific cost-effectiveness criteria under Executive Order No. 6.
grants.description: Executive Order No. 6 requires New York State agencies to report Qualified Personal Services Contracts (QPSCs) that are new, renewed, or amended and meet specific criteria. These contracts must have a majority of costs attributable to contractor personnel and incur $1 million or more in personal service costs over a twelve-month period. The purpose is to ensure cost-effectiveness and transparency in state contracts. Copies of contracts can be obtained via a Freedom of Information Law (FOIL) request. Interested parties can contact the Records Access Office for more information.
grants.award_text: Minimum $1 million in personal service costs over a twelve-month period
grants.eligibility_notes: Eligible applicants are non-State entities entering into contracts with New York State agencies for personal services.
key_requirements: Contract must be new, renewed, or amended after August 4, 2008.
Majority of costs must be attributable to contractor personnel.
Personal service costs must exceed $1 million over a twelve-month period.

grants.title: Minority and Women-Owned Business Enterprises (M/WBEs)
grants.summary: This grant supports the advancement and utilization of Minority and Women-Owned Business Enterprises (M/WBEs) in New York State through compliance with state participation goals.
grants.description: The Department of Health (DOH) is committed to increasing the utilization of Minority and Women-Owned Business Enterprises (M/WBEs) by supporting the program and awarded vendors. The grant aligns with New York State Executive Law, Article 15A, which mandates state agencies to establish business and employment participation goals for minorities and women on state contracts. Eligible businesses must apply online through Empire State Development (ESD) to become NYS Certified M/WBEs. Once certified, businesses are added to the NYS Directory of Certified Firms, making them visible for state contracting opportunities. Resources such as procurement guidelines and the Grants Gateway portal are available to assist applicants.
grants.eligibility_notes: Eligible applicants are businesses owned by minorities or women that meet the certification requirements of Empire State Development (ESD).

grants.title: Service-Disabled Veteran Owned Business (SDVOB)
grants.summary: This grant supports service-disabled veteran-owned businesses in New York State by providing certification and procurement opportunities.
grants.description: The Service-Disabled Veteran-Owned Business Act, signed into law on May 12, 2014, enables eligible veteran business owners to become certified as a New York State Service-Disabled Veteran-Owned Business (SDVOB). The program aims to increase SDVOB utilization in state procurement activities, with a 6% participation goal. The Department of Health supports the program by creating connections and promoting opportunities for certified vendors. Certification is managed by the OGS Division of Service-Disabled Veterans' Business Development. Interested applicants can visit the provided website for more information on certification and participation.
grants.eligibility_notes: Eligible applicants are service-disabled veteran-owned businesses certified by the OGS Division of Service-Disabled Veterans' Business Development in New York State.
sdg_alignment: SDG 8: Decent Work and Economic Growth

grants.summary: This grant funds the procurement and distribution of naloxone and fentanyl test strips to prevent overdoses.
grants.description: The Naloxone and Fentanyl Strip Overdose Prevention grant aims to reduce overdose deaths by providing access to life-saving naloxone and fentanyl test strips. The program is managed by the New York State Department of Health and supports organizations involved in overdose prevention efforts. Interested applicants must meet mandatory qualifications and submit bids by the specified deadline. Detailed specifications, delivery requirements, and administrative guidelines are outlined in the Invitation for Bids document.
grants.eligibility_notes: Organizations involved in overdose prevention efforts are eligible to apply.
key_requirements: Organizations must be involved in overdose prevention efforts.
Applicants must meet mandatory qualifications outlined in the IFB document.
Bids must be submitted by the specified deadline.

grants.title: Preventive Maintenance, Filter Exchange and Repair Services for Deionized Water Systems and Equipment at The Wadsworth Center
grants.summary: This grant funds preventive maintenance, filter exchange, and repair services for deionized water systems and equipment at the Wadsworth Center.
grants.description: The New York State Department of Health invites bids for preventive maintenance, filter exchange, and repair services for deionized water systems and equipment at the Wadsworth Center. The purpose is to ensure the proper functioning of critical water systems. Eligible bidders must meet mandatory qualifications and attend a mandatory site visit. Applications must be submitted in accordance with the detailed specifications provided in the Invitation for Bids document.
grants.eligibility_notes: Organizations with mandatory qualifications and attendance at the mandatory site visit are eligible to apply.
key_requirements: Mandatory site visit attendance
Compliance with detailed bid specifications
Submission of required bid documents

grants.title: Epidemiologic and Program Evaluation Staffing
grants.summary: This grant funds staffing services to place professional research and epidemiologic staff in the New York State Department of Health Central Office.
grants.description: The New York State Department of Health is seeking competitive bids from qualified organizations to provide staffing services for up to fifteen professional research and epidemiologic staff. These staff will supplement the capacity of units charged with critical research, evaluation, and surveillance functions at the AIDS Institute. The contract term is up to five years, subject to the Department's need for services, availability of funds, and acceptable performance. Interested organizations must submit bids by the specified deadline.
grants.eligibility_notes: Qualified organizations capable of providing staffing services for professional research and epidemiologic staff.
eligible_applicant_types: Organizations specializing in research and epidemiology
key_requirements: Qualified organization capable of providing staffing services
Ability to place up to fifteen professional research and epidemiologic staff
Experience in public health research and evaluation

grants.title: Hunger Prevention and Nutrition Assistance Program Transportation Project
grants.summary: This grant funds transportation projects to support hunger prevention and nutrition assistance programs in New York State.
grants.description: The Hunger Prevention and Nutrition Assistance Program Transportation Project aims to enhance food transportation services to support hunger prevention and nutrition assistance initiatives. Eligible applicants must meet specific qualifications outlined in the bid documents. Applications must be submitted in accordance with the detailed specifications provided. The program is designed to benefit organizations working to reduce food insecurity and improve access to nutritious food. Interested parties can apply by submitting bids as per the instructions in the Invitation for Bids document.
grants.award_text: $320,000 minimum for food transportation funding; $80,000 maximum for administrative funding
grants.eligibility_notes: Organizations involved in hunger prevention and nutrition assistance programs are eligible to apply.
key_requirements: Must be an organization involved in hunger prevention or nutrition assistance
Must meet mandatory qualifications outlined in the bid documents
Must submit bids by the specified deadline

grants.title: Request for Information: Physical Medicine and Rehabilitation Residency Program
grants.summary: This grant seeks information from vendors experienced in providing Physical Medicine and Rehabilitation Residency programs to enhance patient care in acute physical rehabilitation settings.
grants.description: The New York State Department of Health (NYSDOH) and Helen Hayes Hospital are seeking information from vendors with experience in providing Physical Medicine and Rehabilitation Residency programs. These programs aim to ensure high-quality care for patients with acute physical rehabilitation needs by utilizing residents trained in innovative medical approaches. Interested parties should be academic institutions with Accreditation Council for Graduate Medical Education Residency programs and experience working with New York State-owned healthcare institutions. Responses to this RFI will help NYSDOH gather information for future planning and procurement opportunities.
grants.eligibility_notes: Eligible applicants are academic institutions with Accreditation Council for Graduate Medical Education Residency programs and at least five years of experience working with New York State-owned healthcare institutions in the field of Physical Medicine and Rehabilitation.
key_requirements: Academic institutions with Accreditation Council for Graduate Medical Education Residency programs
At least five years of experience working with New York State-owned healthcare institutions
Knowledge in the specialized field of Physical Medicine and Rehabilitation
History of placing residents in New York State PM&R Residency programs

grants.title: Request for Information: Clinical Physician Staffing Services
grants.summary: This RFI seeks information from vendors experienced in providing clinical physician staffing services for acute physical rehabilitation healthcare institutions in New York State.
grants.description: The New York State Department of Health (NYSDOH) Helen Hayes Hospital is seeking information from vendors with experience in providing clinical physician staffing services for its acute physical rehabilitation inpatient and subacute population. The hospital requires highly specialized physicians, including Physiatrists, Neurologists, Internal Medicine Physicians, Pathology Physicians, and Medical Directors, who are licensed in New York State and board-certified in their specialties. Responses to this RFI will help NYSDOH gather information for future planning and procurement processes. Interested parties must submit responses electronically, including company details and relevant experience.
grants.eligibility_notes: Organizations with at least five years of experience providing clinical physician staffing services to New York State-owned acute physical rehabilitation healthcare institutions are eligible.
key_requirements: Five years of experience providing clinical physician staffing services
Physicians must be licensed in New York State
Physicians must be board-certified specialists
Experience in oversight of resident physicians
Academic appointment for physicians

grants.title: Request for Information: Development of an Informational Online Platform for Healthcare Workers
grants.summary: This grant seeks information and ideas for developing a centralized online platform to support healthcare workforce development in New York State.
grants.description: The New York State Department of Health, Office of Healthcare Workforce Innovation, is requesting information to develop and maintain an online platform that connects individuals to healthcare career resources. The platform aims to address workforce shortages by providing access to training, education, employment opportunities, and support services. It will focus on underserved communities and promote diversity in healthcare careers. Interested parties should submit ideas for platform design, outreach strategies, and analytics systems. Responses must be submitted electronically by the specified deadline.
grants.eligibility_notes: Organizations with experience in web platform development, healthcare education, workforce development, and public sector compliance are eligible.
sdg_alignment: SDG 8: Decent Work and Economic Growth
key_requirements: Experience in designing and deploying user-friendly web platforms
Expertise in healthcare education and workforce development
Ability to implement robust analytics systems
Familiarity with New York State procurement processes
Compliance with public sector standards

grants.title: Approved Food Items for the New York State WIC Program
grants.summary: This grant seeks information to identify food items eligible for purchase by NYS WIC participants under updated USDA guidelines.
grants.description: The New York State WIC Program provides nutrition education, nutritious foods, breastfeeding support, and referrals to over 450,000 income-eligible women, infants, and children monthly. This Request for Information (RFI) aims to gather details about food items that meet updated USDA nutritional requirements and align with the revised WIC Food Packages Final Rule. Manufacturers and distributors of eligible food products are encouraged to submit information for consideration. Submission does not guarantee inclusion in the Approved Product List (APL). Responses will assist in program planning and updating the NYS WIC Acceptable Foods Card.
grants.eligibility_notes: Manufacturers and distributors of food items meeting specifications listed in the RFI are eligible to respond.
key_requirements: Food items must meet USDA nutritional requirements.
Products must align with the updated WIC Food Packages Final Rule.
Submission of information does not guarantee inclusion in the Approved Product List.
Respondents must provide detailed product specifications.

grants.title: Request for Information: American Indian Health Program (AIHP) Claims Processing Solutions
grants.summary: This RFI seeks information on software solutions to automate claims processing for the American Indian Health Program (AIHP).
grants.description: The New York State Department of Health (NYSDOH) is requesting information from vendors and interested parties regarding software solutions to assist with automating claims processing for the American Indian Health Program (AIHP). The program serves approximately 25,000 enrolled members of nine Native American nations in New York State, processing 5,000-10,000 claims monthly. The goal is to streamline processes, minimize manual intervention, and improve efficiency. This RFI is not a procurement request but aims to identify technologies available in the market to inform future decisions. Responses should address technical design, customization, interoperability, and implementation capabilities.
grants.eligibility_notes: Organizations with experience in implementing automated healthcare claims processing solutions or adaptable non-healthcare claims processing solutions are eligible.
key_requirements: Experience in automated healthcare claims processing solutions
Ability to customize software for specific functions
Capability to implement solutions spanning the entire claims process
Interoperability with current and future systems

grants.title: Consultant Services to Inform Strategies to Address the Institutions for Mental Diseases (IMDs) Medicaid Exclusion as it Relates to Congregate Residential Programs and the Development of Community Based Services for Youth in Child Welfare
grants.summary: This RFI seeks information from vendors to assist New York State in addressing Medicaid exclusions for Institutions for Mental Diseases (IMDs) and developing community-based services for youth in child welfare.
grants.description: The New York State Department of Health (DOH), with support from the Office of Children and Family Services (OCFS), is soliciting information to develop strategies for addressing Medicaid exclusions for Institutions for Mental Diseases (IMDs). The purpose is to transform systems to provide trauma-focused, community-based services for children and families, avoiding institutional placements whenever possible. Vendors are expected to provide technical assistance, develop strategic plans, and implement operational changes to support child welfare agencies determined to be IMDs. Responses should include solutions for compliance with federal regulations, funding strategies, and stakeholder engagement. This RFI does not involve funding awards.
grants.eligibility_notes: Organizations providing congregate residential services to youth in foster care or other residential placements may respond.
key_requirements: Organizations must provide congregate residential services to youth.
Organizations must address IMD Medicaid exclusion criteria.
Organizations must engage stakeholders in developing transition strategies.

grants.title: Enterprise Electronic Disease & Condition Surveillance (EDSS) Solutions
grants.summary: This grant seeks information on solutions for scalable, systematic, and continuous public health surveillance systems.
grants.description: The purpose of this grant is to gather information on enterprise-level public health surveillance solutions that meet CDC and NYSDOH requirements for integrated, interoperable systems. These systems should support disease tracking, case investigation, contact tracing, and data analytics. Eligible solutions must align with CDC standards, including electronic case reporting, laboratory reporting, and integration of health information databases. Responses should address technical capabilities, interoperability, analytics, security, and implementation. This is a Request for Information (RFI) and not a funding opportunity.
grants.eligibility_notes: Organizations with expertise in public health surveillance systems and compliance with CDC standards are eligible to respond.
key_requirements: Compliance with CDC standards for electronic disease surveillance systems
Integration of health information databases
Support for electronic case and laboratory reporting
Interoperability with state, tribal, local, and territorial partners

grants.title: Request for Information for Vehicle Evaluations and Modifications
grants.summary: This RFI seeks vendor input to assist the New York State Department of Health with vehicle evaluation and modification services for enrollees of various state-administered programs.
grants.description: The New York State Department of Health is requesting information from vendors to better understand solutions for vehicle evaluation and modification services for enrollees of programs such as the Medical Indemnity Fund, Children’s Waiver, Nursing Home Transition and Diversion Waiver, and Traumatic Brain Injury Waiver. The RFI aims to determine vendor interest, understand evaluation processes, identify common modification requests, and gather insights into industry trends. Vendors are encouraged to provide detailed submissions regarding their experience, certifications, processes, and cost estimates. Responses will help the Department improve services and ensure compliance with program regulations.
grants.eligibility_notes: Qualified vendors with experience in vehicle modification evaluations and certifications such as those from the National Mobility Equipment Dealers Association (NMEDA) or ACCES-VR qualifications.
key_requirements: Experience in vehicle modification evaluations
Certifications/licenses such as NMEDA or ACCES-VR qualifications
Ability to provide detailed evaluation reports
Compliance with NYS program regulations
Capability to track and manage communication with enrollees and stakeholders

grants.title: Request for Information for Uniform Tasking Tool (UTT) Solutions
grants.summary: This RFI seeks information on tasking tool solutions to standardize service utilization for Medicaid members requiring personal care services.
grants.description: The New York State Department of Health (NYSDOH) is soliciting information from vendors and interested parties regarding tasking tool solutions and algorithms that use outputs from the interRAI Community Health Assessment (CHA). These tools aim to standardize the determination of personal care services (PCS) and consumer-directed personal assistance services (CDPAS) hours for Medicaid members. The goal is to ensure consistency in care plans for members with similar needs. Feedback is especially sought from vendors with established solutions capable of using CHA outputs. Responses will help identify available options, advantages, disadvantages, and implementation requirements.
grants.eligibility_notes: Organizations with established tasking tool solutions or algorithms capable of using outputs from the interRAI CHA are eligible to respond.
key_requirements: Established tasking tool solutions or algorithms
Capability to use outputs from the interRAI Community Health Assessment (CHA)
Ability to standardize service utilization for Medicaid members

grants.title: Request for Information for Innovative Medicaid Enterprise Systems (MES) Solutions
grants.summary: This RFI seeks information on innovative solutions for Medicaid Enterprise Systems to support long-term planning and development.
grants.description: The New York State Department of Health (DOH) is engaged in a multi-year effort to create a Medicaid Enterprise Systems Roadmap. This roadmap will guide the design, development, and implementation of Medicaid Enterprise Systems, including Medicaid Management Information Systems (MMIS), Medicaid Eligibility and Enrollment (E&E) modules, and supporting services. The RFI aims to gather information on solutions that address emerging challenges such as value-based payments, social determinants of health, and interoperability standards. Responses may be shared with CMS, contractors, and other states. Interested parties should identify proprietary information for confidential handling.
grants.eligibility_notes: Organizations with expertise in Medicaid Enterprise Systems solutions are eligible to respond.
key_requirements: Expertise in Medicaid Enterprise Systems solutions
Ability to address emerging challenges in Medicaid programs
Compliance with ONC and CMS standards

grants.title: Solicitation of Interest #20713/SFS#NANYSA1 – Nurses Across New York Loan Repayment Program – State Agencies- Cycle 1 (NANY SA1)
grants.summary: This grant funds loan repayment for registered and licensed practical nurses who commit to working in underserved areas of New York State for three years.
grants.description: The Nurses Across New York Loan Repayment Program (NANY) aims to recruit and retain registered professional nurses and licensed practical nurses in underserved communities across New York State. The program provides funding to repay qualified educational debt in exchange for a three-year service commitment in medically underserved areas. Eligible nurses must work full-time providing direct patient care in approved health care facilities or organizations under the jurisdiction of specific New York State agencies. Applications are limited per nurse and per facility, and funding is allocated proportionally between New York City and the rest of the state.
grants.eligibility_notes: Eligible applicants are registered professional nurses or licensed practical nurses licensed to practice in New York State, in good standing, and committed to working full-time in underserved areas for three years. Nurse practitioners and nurses employed by federal facilities are not eligible.
key_requirements: Must be a U.S. citizen or permanent resident alien.
Must be licensed to practice as a registered nurse or licensed practical nurse in New York State.
Must commit to a three-year service obligation in an underserved area.
Must provide full-time direct patient care (minimum 144 hours per month).
Cannot be employed by federal health care facilities.

grants.title: Solicitation of Interest #20652 – Doctors Across New York Physician Loan Repayment and Physician Practice Support Programs– Cycle XI
grants.summary: This grant funds programs to recruit and retain physicians in medically underserved areas of New York State.
grants.description: The New York State Doctors Across New York (DANY) initiative aims to train and place physicians in underserved areas to improve access to quality healthcare and promote health equity. The Physician Loan Repayment (PLR) and Physician Practice Support (PPS) programs provide funding in exchange for a three-year service commitment in underserved areas. Physicians can use funds to repay educational debt, establish or join medical practices, or receive enhanced compensation. Applications are open to eligible physicians and healthcare facilities.
grants.award_text: Up to $40,000 per year for three years (not to exceed $120,000 total)
grants.eligibility_notes: Eligible applicants include physicians (MD or DO) licensed in New York State and healthcare facilities located in underserved areas. Physicians must meet specific criteria, including U.S. citizenship or permanent residency, good standing, and full-time clinical practice.
key_requirements: Physician must be licensed to practice medicine in New York State.
Physician must commit to a three-year service obligation in an underserved area.
Physician must provide full-time clinical practice (minimum 32 clinical hours per week).
Physician must not have previously received DANY funding.
Physician must not be in breach of other health professional service obligations.

grants.title: Solicitation of Interest #20643 – Primary Care Service Corps – Round 7
grants.summary: This grant funds loan repayment for clinicians working in underserved areas of New York State.
grants.description: The Primary Care Service Corps (PCSC) Loan Repayment Program, issued by the New York State Department of Health, aims to increase the supply of certain clinicians in underserved areas. Eligible clinicians include dentists, dental hygienists, nurse practitioners, physician assistants, certified nurse midwives, clinical psychologists, licensed clinical social workers, licensed marriage and family therapists, and licensed mental health counselors. Applicants must commit to a three-year service obligation at an NHSC Approved Site and meet specific licensing and employment requirements. Up to $1,000,000 in funding is available, with individual awards ranging from $36,000 to $72,000 depending on the clinician type. Applications are due by May 8, 2025.
grants.award_text: Up to $72,000 for dentists, $54,000 for primary care clinicians, and $36,000 for other eligible clinicians.
grants.eligibility_notes: Eligible applicants include dentists, dental hygienists, nurse practitioners, physician assistants, certified nurse midwives, clinical psychologists, licensed clinical social workers, licensed marriage and family therapists, and licensed mental health counselors who meet licensing and employment requirements.
key_requirements: Must be licensed to practice in New York State.
Must commit to a three-year service obligation at an NHSC Approved Site.
Must not have overlapping obligations under other loan repayment programs.
Must meet weekly work hour requirements.
Must be in good standing with no professional license revocations or felony convictions.

grants.title: Solicitation of Interest # 20633 - Hepatitis C Testing Supplies Purchasing, Storage and Distribution
grants.summary: This grant funds the establishment of a program to purchase, store, track, and distribute hepatitis C testing supplies for underinsured populations in New York State.
grants.description: The New York State Department of Health AIDS Institute is offering funding for a not-for-profit organization to create a program for purchasing, tracking, storing, and distributing hepatitis C testing supplies. The program aims to support the NYS Hepatitis C Testing Program, which provides free testing supplies to public health programs and community-based organizations serving underinsured populations at high risk for hepatitis C. Applicants must demonstrate capacity to manage logistics and distribution statewide. Interested organizations can apply by following the instructions provided in the solicitation documents.
grants.eligibility_notes: Eligible applicants are not-for-profit organizations capable of managing statewide logistics for hepatitis C testing supplies.
key_requirements: Must be a not-for-profit organization
Capacity to manage statewide logistics
Experience in public health or community-based programs
Ability to serve underinsured populations
Expertise in hepatitis C prevention and care

grants.title: Solicitation of Interest # C041225 – Scientific and Education Research Peer Reviews
grants.summary: This grant sought qualified organizations to provide independent peer reviews of funding applications for breast cancer and spinal cord injury research projects.
grants.description: The Solicitation of Interest (SOI) aimed to identify organizations with the qualifications to conduct independent scientific and technical merit peer reviews of funding applications received by the New York State Department of Health. The peer review services were intended for breast cancer scientific and education research projects and spinal cord injury scientific research projects. The Department planned to utilize a single organization to provide these services. However, the SOI was canceled effective January 23, 2025. Interested parties were expected to meet specific qualifications and provide detailed proposals.
grants.eligibility_notes: Qualified organizations with expertise in scientific and technical merit peer reviews for breast cancer and spinal cord injury research projects.
key_requirements: Expertise in scientific and technical merit peer reviews
Experience with breast cancer and spinal cord injury research projects
Ability to provide independent and unbiased reviews

grants.title: Solicitation of Interest # C041205 - Vital Events Integrated System
grants.summary: This grant seeks qualified organizations to provide a comprehensive and integrated vital records solution for the New York State Department of Health.
grants.description: The New York State Department of Health (NYSDOH) is seeking vendors to develop, test, and implement a comprehensive vital records system. This system will manage electronic registration and issuance of vital events such as births, deaths, marriages, divorces, and adoptions. The solution must integrate seamlessly with existing systems, support secure data collection and processing, and comply with state and national guidelines. Interested organizations must provide software, infrastructure, and integration capabilities, and ensure secure hosting and maintenance of the system. Applications must be submitted by the specified deadline.
grants.eligibility_notes: Qualified organizations with the functionality to provide a comprehensive vital records solution.
sdg_alignment: SDG 16: Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions
key_requirements: Ability to provide a comprehensive vital records solution
Integration with existing NYSDOH systems
Compliance with state and national guidelines
Secure hosting and maintenance of the system
No subcontractors allowed

grants.title: Solicitation of Interest # C040086 - Lead Rental Registry
grants.summary: Funds the development and implementation of a statewide rental registry program to identify and remediate lead hazards in New York State.
grants.description: This grant supports the creation of a statewide rental registry program aimed at eliminating childhood lead exposure in New York State. The program will identify residential dwellings with two or more units built before 1980 in high-risk areas, mandate routine inspections for lead hazards, and ensure remediation of identified lead hazards. The grant funds the design, development, and maintenance of a data system to manage the registry, track inspections, and provide reporting tools. Eligible organizations must demonstrate experience in managing housing data and contracting with government entities. Applications must include a cover sheet and a four-page document detailing organizational capacity and experience, submitted via email.
grants.eligibility_notes: Eligible organizations must have at least five years of experience managing housing data and contracting with government entities.
eligible_applicant_types: Organizations with experience in housing data management and government contracting
key_requirements: Demonstrated ability to design and implement data systems for housing registries
Five years of experience managing housing data
Five years of experience contracting with government entities
Capacity to provide training and ongoing system support

grants.title: Solicitation of Interest # 20526 – Nurses Across New York Loan Repayment Program – Cycle 2
grants.summary: Funds to recruit and retain registered and licensed practical nurses in underserved areas of New York State.
grants.description: The Nurses Across New York Loan Repayment Program provides funding to registered professional nurses and licensed practical nurses in exchange for a three-year commitment to work in medically underserved areas of New York State. The program aims to address the nursing shortage by offering loan repayment awards to eligible nurses or healthcare facilities to recruit and retain nurses. Applicants must meet specific eligibility criteria, including licensure, good standing, and employment in underserved areas. Applications are open to both newly licensed and experienced nurses, with funding distributed based on service obligations.
grants.award_text: $10,000 for licensed practical nurses; $25,000 for registered nurses
grants.eligibility_notes: Eligible applicants include registered professional nurses and licensed practical nurses licensed in New York State, as well as healthcare facilities recruiting or retaining nurses in underserved areas.
key_requirements: Must be licensed and in good standing in New York State
Must commit to a three-year service obligation in an underserved area
Must provide full-time direct clinical patient care
Must not be in breach of other service obligations
Must not have judgment liens or delinquent child support payments

grants.title: Solicitation of Interest #20472 – Doctors Across New York Physician Loan Repayment and Physician Practice Support Programs– Cycle X
grants.summary: Funds to recruit and retain physicians in medically underserved areas of New York State.
grants.description: The New York State Doctors Across New York (DANY) initiative provides funding to train and place physicians in underserved communities across New York State. The Physician Loan Repayment (PLR) and Physician Practice Support (PPS) programs offer up to $40,000 per year for three years (not exceeding $120,000 total) to physicians who commit to working in underserved areas for a three-year period. Funding can be used to repay educational debt, establish or join medical practices, or provide enhanced compensation. Applications are open to physicians and healthcare facilities meeting specific eligibility criteria.
grants.award_text: Up to $40,000 per year for three years (not exceeding $120,000 total)
grants.eligibility_notes: Eligible applicants include physicians licensed to practice in New York State and healthcare facilities located in underserved areas. Physicians must meet specific qualifications, including U.S. citizenship or permanent residency, medical school graduation, and good standing.
key_requirements: Physician must be licensed to practice in New York State.
Physician must commit to a three-year service obligation in an underserved area.
Physician must not have previously received DANY funding.
Physician must not be in breach of any health professional service obligations.
Physician must provide full-time clinical practice.

grants.title: Supplemental Support to Ryan White Programs
grants.summary: This grant provides funding to organizations impacted by the loss of 340B pharmacy claim revenue due to changes in Medicaid Managed Care in New York State.
grants.description: The New York State Department of Health AIDS Institute is offering funding to organizations that receive Ryan White funding and are affected by the transition of the pharmacy benefit from Medicaid Managed Care to fee-for-service. Eligible organizations must complete an electronic survey to determine their eligibility. The survey requires detailed information about the organization's ability to bill Medicaid directly, operating budget, and 340B-related data. Funding is contingent on the availability of state funds and the number of eligible applicants.
grants.eligibility_notes: Organizations that receive any Ryan White funding and are impacted by the loss of 340B pharmacy claim revenue are eligible to apply.
key_requirements: Must receive Ryan White funding
Must be impacted by the loss of 340B pharmacy claim revenue
Survey must be signed and submitted by an authorized signatory
Only one survey submission per organization
Complete all required survey questions and uploads

grants.title: NY State of Health Private Pay Home Care Services Pilot Program: Phase Two
grants.summary: This grant invites New York State Licensed Home Care Services Agencies (LHCSAs) to participate in a pilot program to provide private pay home care services in eight counties, aiming to improve access to personal care services and reduce reliance on Medicaid.
grants.description: The NY State of Health Private Pay Home Care Services Pilot Program aims to provide New Yorkers with a trusted source to select private pay home care services from licensed agencies. The program facilitates the review and selection of home care aides based on consumer preferences, such as location, language, and gender identity. Eligible LHCSAs must meet specific licensing and compliance requirements to participate. Consumers can access the program through the NY State of Health website and directly arrange services with participating agencies. Payment for services is made directly by the consumer to the agency.
grants.eligibility_notes: Eligible applicants are New York State Licensed Home Care Services Agencies (LHCSAs) that are licensed and registered under Article 36 of the NY Public Health Law, in good standing with the Department of Health, and comply with state and federal wage and hour laws.
key_requirements: Must be licensed and registered under Article 36 of the NY Public Health Law.
Must be in good standing with the Department of Health regarding licensing and compliance.
Must comply with state and federal wage and hour laws.
Must be licensed to provide personal care and home health aide services.

grants.title: Request for Applications for Adult Day Health Care Program Slots in Tompkins and Niagara Counties
grants.summary: This grant funds 25 Adult Day Health Care Program slots for licensed nursing homes in Tompkins and Niagara counties.
grants.description: The New York State Department of Health is soliciting applications from licensed nursing homes in Tompkins and Niagara counties to establish Adult Day Health Care Programs (ADHCP). The initiative is based on recommendations from the Berger Commission Report and aims to improve access to health care services in these counties. Applicants must comply with relevant state regulations and guidelines, and applications will be competitively reviewed and scored based on financial feasibility, program and staffing, compliance, construction, and geographic location. Applications must be complete and submitted by the deadline to be considered.
grants.eligibility_notes: Only licensed nursing home operators in Tompkins and Niagara counties may apply.
key_requirements: Licensed nursing home operator
Compliance with state, federal, and local codes
Submission of complete application by deadline
Adherence to relevant sections of 10 NYCRR regulations
Demonstration of financial feasibility and program staffing

grants.title: Assisted Living Program 3400 Initiative
grants.summary: This grant funds the addition of 3,400 Assisted Living Program beds to provide supportive housing and home care services for individuals eligible for nursing facility placement but who can be served in less restrictive settings.
grants.description: The Assisted Living Program 3400 Initiative, authorized under Section 461-1 of the Social Services Law, aims to expand the availability of Assisted Living Program (ALP) beds in New York State. ALP provides supportive housing and home care services to individuals who are medically eligible for nursing facility placement but whose needs can be met in a less restrictive and lower-cost residential setting. Eligible residents must not require continual nursing care or pose safety risks to others. The program includes personal care, room, board, housekeeping, supervision, home health aides, therapy services, medical supplies, and case management. Applications must follow the Opportunity for Development (OFD) guidelines and include required documentation.
grants.eligibility_notes: Organizations with the capacity to provide adult home services and meet ALP requirements are eligible to apply.
key_requirements: Must provide adult home services
Must meet ALP requirements for staffing and case management
Must ensure health, safety, and well-being of residents

grants.title: Preventive Health and Health Services Block Grant
grants.summary: Funds public health prevention programs in New York State with demonstrated need and alignment to Healthy People 2020 and Department of Health Prevention Agenda objectives.
grants.description: The Preventive Health and Health Services (PHHS) Block Grant provides flexible funding for New York State's public health programs. It supports prevention programs that address demonstrated needs and align with Healthy People 2020 and Department of Health Prevention Agenda objectives. PHHS dollars are distributed to communities through various programs listed on the Department's Grants/Funding Opportunities page. Applicants can find more information on the federal program via the CDC's website. The grant aims to protect public health by addressing local priorities effectively.
grants.eligibility_notes: Organizations in New York State with public health prevention programs that demonstrate need and align with Healthy People 2020 and Department of Health Prevention Agenda objectives.
key_requirements: Demonstrated need for funding
Alignment with Healthy People 2020 objectives
Alignment with Department of Health Prevention Agenda objectives

grants.title: New York State Child and Adult Care Food Program (CACFP)
grants.summary: This grant funds nutrition programs for children and adults in care facilities across New York State.
grants.description: The New York State Child and Adult Care Food Program (CACFP) is an entitlement program funded by the U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA) to promote health and well-being through nutrition. It supports over 1,270 sponsoring organizations and 10,870 licensed or registered care sites, serving over 200,000 children and adults daily. Eligible sponsors include child care centers, home-based family and group child care, community-based adult day care centers, after-school programs, and homeless shelters. Funding is based on household income, and applications must meet income eligibility guidelines. Interested applicants can contact the New York State Department of Health for more information.
grants.eligibility_notes: Eligible organizations include child care centers, home-based family and group child care providers, community-based adult day care centers, after-school programs, and homeless shelters.

grants.title: Locating and Applying for Health Promotion Funds
grants.summary: This guide helps organizations locate, apply for, and obtain funding for health promotion projects.
grants.description: The document provides detailed guidance on finding and applying for health promotion funding from public and private sources, including grants and contracts. It explains the differences between funding types, outlines proposal preparation steps, and offers tips for selecting appropriate funding sources. It also includes information on government RFAs and RFPs, foundation grants, and corporate funding. The guide is designed to assist nonprofit organizations in securing funding for community-benefiting health initiatives.
grants.eligibility_notes: Nonprofit organizations are the primary recipients of most grants and contracts.

grants.title: Drinking Water State Revolving Fund (DWSRF)
grants.summary: Provides financial incentives for public water systems to improve drinking water infrastructure.
grants.description: The Drinking Water State Revolving Fund (DWSRF) offers financial assistance to public water systems for infrastructure improvements such as treatment plants, distribution mains, and storage facilities. The program is guided by the Safe Drinking Water Act, requiring the state to prepare an annual Intended Use Plan (IUP) that outlines funding priorities and eligible projects. Applicants must submit an IUP Project Listing Form and an acceptable engineering report to be considered for funding. Certain projects may qualify for special financing under the Disadvantaged Community Program, including interest-free loans or principal forgiveness.
grants.eligibility_notes: Eligible applicants include public water systems and municipalities seeking funding for drinking water infrastructure projects.
key_requirements: Submission of an IUP Project Listing Form
Submission of an acceptable engineering report
Compliance with the Safe Drinking Water Act
Projects must address priority issues outlined in the IUP

grants.title: Sexual Assault Forensic Examiner (SAFE) Program
grants.summary: This program certifies hospitals in New York State to provide specialized care for sexual assault patients.
grants.description: The Sexual Assault Forensic Examiner (SAFE) Program, established by the New York State Department of Health (DOH), certifies hospitals to provide victim-centered care for sexual assault patients. SAFE-designated hospitals must meet specific standards, including timely care, forensic evidence collection, and emotional support for victims. Hospitals licensed under Article 28 of the Public Health Law can apply for SAFE designation at any time. The program aims to ensure quality medical care, compliance with federal and state requirements, and ongoing staff training. Applications and agreements are available through the Bureau of Perinatal, Reproductive, and Sexual Health (BPRASH).
grants.eligibility_notes: Hospitals licensed under Article 28 of the Public Health Law in New York State are eligible to apply.
key_requirements: Licensed under Article 28 of the Public Health Law
Compliance with SAFE Program standards
Provision of victim-centered care
Forensic evidence collection and storage
Staff training and quality assurance

grants.title: Consumer Directed Personal Assistance Program (CDPAP)
grants.summary: A New York State Medicaid program allowing eligible recipients to hire and train their own caregivers for home care services.
grants.description: The Consumer Directed Personal Assistance Program (CDPAP) empowers Medicaid-eligible individuals to receive care at home from caregivers they choose and train, including friends or family members (excluding spouses, designated representatives, or parents of consumers under 21). It serves chronically ill or physically disabled individuals needing help with daily activities or skilled nursing services. Applicants must meet Medicaid eligibility, have a stable medical condition, and fulfill Minimum Needs Requirements. The program offers flexibility in caregiver selection and requires recipients to manage administrative tasks such as payroll and training. Applications are coordinated through Public Partnership LLC (PPL), the Statewide Fiscal Intermediary.
grants.eligibility_notes: Medicaid recipients aged 21 or older with a stable medical condition and a need for home care services. Must meet Minimum Needs Requirements and be capable of self-directing or have a designated representative.
key_requirements: Must be eligible for NYS Medicaid
Must have a stable medical condition
Must meet Minimum Needs Requirements if age 21 or older
Must be capable of self-directing or have a designated representative

grants.title: Drinking Water Protection Program
grants.summary: Funds programs to manage, protect, and improve New York’s public drinking water systems.
grants.description: The Drinking Water Protection Program aims to ensure safe and quality drinking water for New Yorkers by managing public water systems that supply nearly 95% of residents. It provides resources for reducing exposure to contaminants, safeguarding household wells and septic systems, and offers training and financial incentives for water supply professionals. Applications for infrastructure financing and certifications are available through the program. The program also collaborates with inter-agency initiatives to address emerging contaminants and local water supply concerns.
grants.eligibility_notes: Organizations managing public water systems or involved in water infrastructure projects in New York State are eligible.
key_requirements: Must be located in New York State
Must manage or support public water systems
Must comply with New York State drinking water regulations

grants.title: Innovation in Behavioral Health (IBH) Model Grant Award
grants.summary: This grant funds innovative models of care to improve behavioral and physical health outcomes for adults enrolled in Medicaid and Medicare with moderate to severe mental health conditions and substance use disorder.
grants.description: The Innovation in Behavioral Health (IBH) Model grant aims to bridge the gap between behavioral and physical health by aligning payment systems between Medicaid and Medicare for integrated services. Specialty behavioral health practices will screen and assess patients for priority health conditions, mental health conditions, and substance use disorder. The grant supports the design and implementation of innovative care models in Western New York counties. Applications are administered in collaboration with the Office of Mental Health (OMH) and the Office of Addiction Services and Supports (OASAS).
grants.award_text: Up to $7.5 million between 2025 and 2032, with $1.25 million awarded for calendar year 2025.
grants.eligibility_notes: State Medicaid agencies and specialty behavioral health practices serving adults enrolled in Medicaid and Medicare with moderate to severe mental health conditions and substance use disorder.
key_requirements: Applicants must be state Medicaid agencies or specialty behavioral health practices.
Focus on improving behavioral and physical health outcomes.
Serve adults enrolled in Medicaid and Medicare with moderate to severe mental health conditions and substance use disorder.

grants.title: Office of Drug User Health Grant
grants.summary: Funds harm reduction initiatives to improve the quality of life for New Yorkers who use drugs.
grants.description: The Office of Drug User Health Grant supports harm reduction practices, accessible healthcare, and supportive services for individuals who use drugs in New York State. It aims to reduce health inequities, fight systemic racism, and empower communities affected by drug use. The grant focuses on evidence-based, non-judgmental, anti-racist, and gender-affirming practices. Applicants can apply to fund initiatives aligned with harm reduction principles, including overdose prevention, HIV/HCV prevention, and community empowerment.
grants.eligibility_notes: Organizations focused on harm reduction, healthcare, and community support for drug users in New York State.
key_requirements: Must be based in New York State
Focus on harm reduction practices
Support individuals who use drugs
Promote health equity and anti-racist initiatives

grants.title: New York State Infertility Reimbursement Program
grants.summary: Provides grant assistance to higher volume providers of infertility services in New York State.
grants.description: The New York State Infertility Reimbursement Program offers grant assistance to approved providers who meet specific eligibility criteria and performance standards for select infertility services. Insured patients whose insurance does not cover or only partially covers these costs can apply for the program through an approved provider. The program aims to support individuals seeking infertility services and reduce financial barriers.
